message.content:
The screenshot shows the **AudioRelay** application open on a Windows laptop, with the interface primarily in **Arabic** (with some English labels). Below is a detailed breakdown of every visible element, section, and interactive component:


### **1. Window Header & Top Bar**
- **Title Bar**: Displays "AudioRelay" (left) and standard Windows window controls (right):  
  - Minimize button (–), Maximize button (□), Close button (×).  
- **Language Toggle**: A button labeled "العربية" (Arabic) in the top-right corner, likely to switch the interface language.  


### **2. Left Panel: Device & Audio Input Section**
This panel contains device information and audio input settings:  
- **Device Identification**:  
  - Text: "DESKTOP-785AIIQ" (device name).  
  - IP Address: "192.168.64.6" (network address).  
- **Audio Input Section**:  
  - Header: "الإدخال الصوتي" (Audio Input, Arabic).  
  - Subtext: "اختر جهاز الإدخال الصوتي" (Choose audio input device, Arabic).  
  - **Dropdown/Selector**: A clickable element labeled "Microphone Array ..." (English) with a right-facing arrow (>) indicating it can be expanded to select a specific microphone.  


### **3. Right Panel: Settings & Help Section**
This panel is divided into three subsections (headers in Arabic) with interactive elements:  

#### **a. "الإعدادات" (Settings) Subsection**  
- Header: "الإعدادات" (Settings, Arabic).  
- Text: "اختر جهاز الإخراج الصوتي الذي تريد استخدامه" (Choose the audio output device you want to use, Arabic).  
- **Icon Button**: A speaker icon (🔊) next to the text, likely to toggle or configure audio output settings.  

#### **b. "الإدخال الصوتي" (Audio Input) Subsection**  
- Header: "الإدخال الصوتي" (Audio Input, Arabic).  
- Text: "اختر جهاز الإدخال الصوتي الذي تريد استخدامه" (Choose the audio input device you want to use, Arabic).  
- **Icon Button**: A microphone icon (🎤) next to the text, likely to toggle or configure audio input settings.  

#### **c. "المساعدة" (Help) Subsection**  
- Header: "المساعدة" (Help, Arabic).  
- Text: "لا يوجد مساعدة متاحة" (No help available, Arabic).  
- **Button**: A checkbox labeled "الإعدادات" (Settings, Arabic) with a checkmark (✓) inside, indicating this option is **currently enabled/selected**.  


### **4. Central Pop-Up Window (Modal)**
A semi-transparent pop-up window is open in the center, with:  
- **Close Button**: An "×" (cross) in the top-left corner to dismiss the pop-up.  
- **Header**: "الإعدادات" (Settings, Arabic) at the top-right of the pop-up.  
- **Content**: Text in Arabic (partially visible) related to settings, with the same "الإعدادات" checkbox button as in the right panel (confirming it is selected).  


### **5. Bottom Taskbar (Windows)**
The Windows taskbar is visible at the bottom, showing:  
- System tray icons (left): Volume, network, battery, etc.  
- Language indicator: "ENG" (English) and a keyboard icon, confirming the system language is English (while the app uses Arabic).  
- Application icons (center): Chrome, File Explorer, and other apps.  
- Start menu button (left) and search bar (center-right).  


### **Interactive Elements Summary**
- **Clickable Buttons**:  
  - Window controls (minimize, maximize, close).  
  - Language toggle ("العربية").  
  - Microphone Array dropdown (left panel).  
  - Speaker icon (audio output settings).  
  - Microphone icon (audio input settings).  
  - "الإعدادات" checkbox (help section, selected).  
  - Pop-up close button (×).  
- **Selected/Enabled**:  
  - "الإعدادات" checkbox (in both the right panel and pop-up, marked with a checkmark).  


### **Layout & Design**
- The interface uses a light blue/gray background with a subtle wave pattern.  
- Sections are clearly divided into left (device info) and right (settings/help) panels.  
- The pop-up window overlays the main interface, suggesting it is a modal dialog for configuration.  


This breakdown covers all visible buttons, toggles, options, and settings, including their purpose, selection state, and layout.
